ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Hainanese Curry Rice. FOODON_00002809 (curry dish) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for Hainanese Curry Rice. A0CKP (rice-based dish) is used as the closest defensible parent. Curry sauce and chicken components are implicit.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for Hainanese Curry Rice as a specific food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Hainan Curry Rice is approximately 400 g, comprising a mound of steamed white rice topped with a generous ladle of chicken curry sauce, typically served on a single plate as a complete meal.

nutrient_source.title: Hainanese Curry Rice — HPB Singapore Foodo / USDA SR Legacy composite estimate for curry rice with chicken
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single HPB Foodo entry exactly matches 'Hainan Curry Rice (Risotto)'. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Foodo entries for Hainanese curry rice and chicken curry with rice, cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy data for steamed white rice (USDA #20444) and chicken curry (USDA #05592). The term 'risotto' in the restaurant menu name is interpreted as a marketing descriptor for a creamy curry rice presentation, not an Italian risotto preparation. Values represent a per-100g estimate for a mixed plate of steamed rice with chicken curry sauce at approximately 55% rice and 45% curry components.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values 2021 — white rice and curry rice analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Hainanese Curry Rice. GI of 65 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white rice (GI ~64–72, mean ~68) moderated downward by the presence of fat and protein from coconut-milk curry sauce, consistent with published GI values for mixed rice-curry meals (GI 60–68). Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 70.0) / 100 = 45.5, using 400 g serving with 17.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g (70 g total carbohydrate per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~320 mg per 100 g; ~1280 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Hainan Curry Rice to occasional consumption and request reduced-salt preparation, as a single 400 g serving may contribute approximately 55% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2300 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~45.5 per serving) driven by refined white rice base
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume Hainan Curry Rice in reduced portions or substitute brown rice where available, as the high glycemic load from refined white rice can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables may help blunt the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat from coconut milk (~2.9 g per 100 g; ~11.6 g per serving)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should be aware that the coconut-milk-based curry sauce contributes a moderate saturated fat load per serving; choosing smaller portions and requesting less coconut milk in preparation can help align intake with the recommended saturated fat limit of less than 20 g per day.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Hainan Curry Rice (also known as Hainanese Curry Rice) is a beloved Singaporean hawker dish with roots in the Hainanese immigrant community. It consists of steamed white rice served with a rich, fragrant curry sauce and tender braised or stewed chicken pieces. The curry sauce is typically made with coconut milk, lemongrass, galangal, turmeric, chilli, curry leaves, onion, and garlic, giving it a creamy, mildly spicy, and aromatic character. The dish is sometimes described as 'risotto-style' in modern restaurant menus to indicate a creamier, more integrated rice-and-sauce presentation, though it is distinct from Italian risotto in both technique and ingredients. Nutritionally, a standard 400 g serving provides approximately 592 kcal, 28.8 g protein, 23.2 g fat (including ~11.6 g saturated fat from coconut milk), and 70 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is high (~1280 mg per serving), making it a concern for individuals with hypertension. The glycemic load is high (~45.5 per serving) due to the refined white rice base, which is clinically relevant for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. The saturated fat from coconut milk is a moderate concern for cardiovascular health. The dish is halal-friendly and dairy-free. It is commonly consumed as a lunch or dinner main course in Singapore hawker centres and casual restaurants.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Hainanese Curry Rice?
What is the GI of curry rice?
Can diabetics eat Hainan Curry Rice?
Is Hainan curry rice the same as risotto?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exactly matches 'Hainan Curry Rice (Risotto)' as served by The Rainbow Bistro. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Foodo Hainanese curry rice entries and USDA SR Legacy data for steamed white rice and chicken curry components. The 'risotto' descriptor is interpreted as a marketing term for a creamier presentation. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for white rice moderated by curry fat and protein content. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component foods are well-documented in approved sources and the adaptation methodology is transparent.
notes: 1. The restaurant menu name 'Hainan Curry Rice (Risotto)' uses 'risotto' as a marketing descriptor for a creamy curry rice presentation; this record treats the dish as Hainanese Curry Rice and not as Italian risotto. 2. Nutrient values are per-100g estimates for a mixed plate of approximately 55% steamed white rice and 45% chicken curry sauce by weight. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo or USDA SR Legacy source entries for this dish category. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo or USDA SR Legacy source entries for this dish category. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entries for this dish category. 6. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed negligible/trace in coconut-milk and chicken-based curry preparations per USDA SR Legacy data. 7. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate; a direct GI measurement for Hainanese Curry Rice has not been published in the University of Sydney GI Database as of the accessed date. 8. The cuisine tag 'Nasi Lemak' in the input record appears to be a misclassification; this dish is Hainanese Curry Rice and is not Nasi Lemak. The cuisine is classified as Singaporean (Hainanese heritage).
